items.label: Abyssinia
items.originNotes: Commonly referenced in Ethiopian lineage discussions.
notes: Treat as lineage context; cup expression still depends on terroir and process.

items.label: Andungsari
items.originNotes: Indonesian selection frequently referenced in Java and East Java production datasets.
notes: Regional lineage context for Indonesian lots.

items.label: Arara
items.originNotes: Brazilian selection increasingly seen in specialty and commercial lots.
notes: Good regional cue for Brazilian lots; verify producer documentation when lot traceability is available.

items.label: Ateng
items.originNotes: Regional name often associated with Sumatran Catimor-derived material.
notes: Regional naming can vary by producer and mill.

items.label: Ateng Super
items.originNotes: Common Indonesian smallholder naming for selected Catimor-line material, especially in Sumatra.
notes: Regional trade naming can vary by producer and mill; treat as taxonomy context first.

items.label: Batian
items.originNotes: Kenyan disease-resistant selection used alongside classic Kenyan lineages.
notes: Treat as Kenyan selection context; acidity and structure still depend on processing and roast development.

items.label: Blue Mountain
items.searchText: blue mountain jamaica blue mountain jbm classic-arabica jamaica papua new guinea hawaii
items.originNotes: Typica-derived commercial and specialty reference associated with Jamaica and regional plantings.
notes: Use as lineage and market label context; avoid assuming Jamaican origin without lot evidence.

items.label: Borbor
items.originNotes: Indonesian regional variety name frequently referenced in North Sumatra lots.
notes: Regional naming can overlap with local lineage aliases and should be validated per lot document.

items.label: Borneo Typica
items.originNotes: Regional Typica naming used for Borneo/Kalimantan coffees in Southeast Asia trade listings.
notes: Regional alias entry; cup behavior depends more on process and roast than alias alone.

items.label: Bourbon
items.originNotes: One of the classic Arabica variety groups.
notes: Classic sweet structure; do not assume a numeric modifier without cup evidence.

items.label: Castillo
items.originNotes: Common Colombian disease-resistant variety.
notes: Planner uses this as metadata, not as a hard extraction shortcut.

items.label: Catimor
items.originNotes: Caturra x Timor hybrid family.
notes: Do not assume lower cup quality; rely on actual lot quality and roast execution.

items.label: Catuai
items.originNotes: Mundo Novo x Caturra cross.
notes: Often appears in blended lot metadata.

items.label: Caturra
items.originNotes: Widely planted Bourbon mutation in Latin America.
notes: Use as taxonomy only unless a future evidence rule is curated.

items.label: Cauvery
items.originNotes: Indian Catimor-line cultivar relevant to South Asia and some Southeast Asia curation contexts.
notes: Useful lineage tag when producers list Cauvery in regional green-coffee documentation.

items.label: Centroamericano H1
items.searchText: centroamericano h1 h1 centroamericano hybrid-f1 costa rica nicaragua el salvador honduras
items.originNotes: F1 hybrid used in Central American production and specialty programs.
notes: Hybrid label improves lot context; no aggressive recipe shift without density or sensory data.

items.label: Chandragiri
items.originNotes: South-Asia Arabica selection known in Indian specialty catalogs and adjacent regional contexts.
notes: Lower-confidence regional taxonomy entry; confirm against lot documents when exact cultivar identity matters.

items.label: Chiroso
items.originNotes: Colombian specialty selection frequently referenced in high-end microlots.
notes: Use as specialty context; keep brew changes driven by roast, density, and target profile.

items.label: Colombia
items.originNotes: Colombian variety family developed for disease resistance.
notes: Often listed at origin level in producer data.

items.label: Conilon
items.searchText: conilon brazil robusta robusta conilon canephora brazil
items.originNotes: Brazilian Canephora/Robusta market label often used for espresso and blends.
notes: Canephora context only; expect different solubility and bitterness risk versus Arabica lots.

items.label: Ethiopian Heirloom
items.originNotes: Commercial umbrella term rather than one discrete cultivar.
notes: Taxonomy only; not a single genetic variety.

items.label: Gayo 1
items.originNotes: Indonesian regional cultivar naming used in Aceh Gayo smallholder and cooperative datasets.
notes: Treat as regional naming context first; lineage can overlap Catimor-derived materials.

items.label: Geisha / Gesha
items.originNotes: Widely referenced high-value Ethiopian-origin selection.
notes: Planner leaves numeric changes conservative unless the cup justifies them.

items.label: IHCAFE 90
items.originNotes: Honduran Catimor-derived variety used in commercial and specialty supply.
notes: Useful commercial variety cue; process and roast should carry most recipe decisions.

items.label: Java
items.originNotes: Historic Typica-related lineage distributed from Java to multiple producing regions.
notes: Classic lineage marker with broad historical relevance in Southeast Asia.

items.label: K7
items.originNotes: East African selection appearing in several Asian specialty transplant programs.
notes: Lower-confidence taxonomy entry; verify producer metadata where possible.

items.label: Kartika 1
items.originNotes: Indonesian Arabica selection associated with highland smallholder programs in Java and Sumatra.
notes: Regional taxonomy reference; validate cultivar naming against lot-level documents.

items.label: Kartika 2
items.originNotes: Companion Indonesian selection often listed alongside Kartika 1 in regional nursery references.
notes: Lower-confidence taxonomy entry pending stronger public varietal references.

items.label: Kent
items.originNotes: Historic Indian Arabica line with direct relevance to South and Southeast Asian coffee history.
notes: Regional historical reference entry; verify lot-level naming when possible.

items.label: Laurina / Bourbon Pointu
items.searchText: laurina / bourbon pointu bourbon pointu low caffeine bourbon specialty-reference reunion brazil central america
items.originNotes: Bourbon mutation known for low caffeine and specialty lots.
notes: Niche specialty label; keep extraction conservative unless lot data shows high solubility.

items.label: Liberica
items.originNotes: Species-level entry used in Southeast Asia menus, especially for Philippines and Malaysia contexts.
notes: Species tag only; planner should rely on cup behavior and roast data for numeric changes.

items.label: Linie S
items.originNotes: Indonesian breeding line used for quality and rust resistance programs.
notes: Use as taxonomy context only unless lot-level data confirms behavior.

items.label: Maragogipe
items.originNotes: Large-bean Typica mutation.
notes: Rare enough that roast and process context matter more than taxonomy alone.

items.label: Marsellesa
items.searchText: marsellesa marsella disease-resistant nicaragua honduras el salvador mexico
items.originNotes: Sarchimor-derived variety used in Central American production.
notes: Good fit for commercial-specialty lots; use sensory result before applying strong variety assumptions.

items.label: Mokka / Moka
items.searchText: mokka / moka moka mocha variety specialty-reference yemen hawaii central america
items.originNotes: Small-bean specialty reference used by some producers and roasters.
notes: Small-bean label can affect grind behavior; confirm with grinder feedback rather than variety name alone.

items.label: Mundo Novo
items.originNotes: Typica x Bourbon lineage.
notes: Taxonomy only.

items.label: Obata
items.originNotes: Brazilian selection used in commercial and specialty production.
notes: Brazilian lineage cue; roast and processing should remain primary brew controls.

items.label: Ombligon
items.originNotes: Colombian specialty selection found in limited microlot offerings.
notes: Microlot context only; avoid numeric assumptions until roast density and sensory data are known.

items.label: P88
items.originNotes: Commonly referenced in Indonesian specialty coffee listings.
notes: Regional descriptor; keep extraction moves conservative until taste confirms.

items.label: Pacamara
items.originNotes: Pacas x Maragogipe hybrid.
notes: Often larger-bean lots; recipe changes should still follow cup taste.

items.label: Pacas
items.originNotes: Bourbon mutation from El Salvador, used as parent material in many modern hybrids.
notes: Useful lineage context when reading hybrid variety pedigrees in import and roasting datasets.

items.label: Parainema
items.originNotes: Honduran Sarchimor-derived variety used in specialty and commercial production.
notes: Disease-resistant variety cue; target profile and taste feedback should drive final dial.

items.label: Pink Bourbon
items.searchText: pink bourbon bourbon rosado rosado specialty-reference colombia
items.originNotes: Specialty market label associated with Colombian and Latin American microlots.
notes: Use as high-value specialty cue; do not infer genetics or brew changes without lot documentation.

items.label: Robusta / Canephora
items.originNotes: Species-level category rather than one variety.
notes: Species tag only; no automatic numeric modifier is applied.

items.label: Ruiru 11
items.originNotes: Kenyan disease-resistant variety common in regional production.
notes: Regional Kenyan cue; process and roast development remain stronger predictors for brew behavior.

items.label: S288
items.originNotes: Historic Indian selection lineage seen in South and Southeast Asia legacy Arabica plantings.
notes: Regional taxonomy context entry for South-Asia-linked offerings; validate lot-level cultivar naming where possible.

items.label: S795
items.originNotes: Widely referenced in Indonesian production.
notes: Regional context only.

items.label: Sarchimor
items.searchText: sarchimor villa sarchi x timor hybrid
items.originNotes: Important Timor-hybrid-derived line used across Latin America and Southeast Asia.
notes: Strongly relevant to Southeast Asia breeding history and modern rust-resistant planting programs.

items.label: Sidra
items.originNotes: Specialty variety/selection commonly associated with Ecuadorian and Colombian high-value lots.
notes: Use as high-value specialty context; keep numeric changes conservative until roast, density, and sensory data confirm solubility.

items.label: Sigararutang
items.originNotes: Indonesian selection frequently seen in Sumatra lots.
notes: Use cup results before moving recipe aggressively.

items.label: SL28
items.originNotes: Scott Agricultural Laboratories selection from Kenya.
notes: Frequently associated with vivid acidity; still use sensory feedback over assumptions.

items.label: SL34
items.originNotes: Scott Agricultural Laboratories selection from Kenya.
notes: Use as descriptive context only.

items.label: SLN5B
items.originNotes: Indian Selection 5B line occasionally referenced by South Asia and ASEAN specialty suppliers.
notes: Lower-confidence regional taxonomy entry pending stronger open varietal documentation.

items.label: SLN6
items.originNotes: Indian Selection 6 lineage seen in South Asia and occasional Southeast Asia extension plantings.
notes: Taxonomy context entry for regional offerings; cup behavior must be validated by roast and process.

items.label: SLN7
items.originNotes: Selection 7 lineage from India that appears in South and Southeast Asia specialty contexts.
notes: Regional taxonomy entry; confirm lot-level cultivar evidence when precision is required.

items.label: SLN9
items.originNotes: Indian Selection 9 lineage frequently referenced in South Asia and some Southeast Asia plantings.
notes: Useful taxonomy tag for South-Asia-linked offerings in Asian specialty roaster menus.

items.label: Tabi
items.originNotes: Colombian variety developed for disease resistance and specialty production.
notes: Good Colombian specialty cue; keep recipe envelope controlled by deterministic planner.

items.label: Timtim
items.originNotes: Timor Hybrid lineage widely referenced across Indonesia and Timor-Leste.
notes: Important parent line for many disease-resistant cultivars in Southeast Asia.

items.label: Typica
items.originNotes: Foundational Arabica lineage.
notes: Use cup result rather than lineage alone to push recipe changes.

items.label: Typica Mejorado
items.searchText: typica mejorado mejorado ecuador mejorado specialty-reference ecuador
items.originNotes: Specialty market label commonly associated with Ecuadorian high-value lots.
notes: Use as specialty lot context; avoid assuming Typica-only behavior without producer documentation.

items.label: USDA 762
items.originNotes: Historic Indian-line selection now seen across South and Southeast Asia.
notes: Used as taxonomy context only unless lot-level evidence supports numeric brew shifts.

items.label: Villa Sarchi
items.originNotes: Bourbon mutation common in Central America.
notes: Planner keeps numeric behavior neutral.

items.label: Wush Wush
items.searchText: wush wush wushwush ethiopian-landrace ethiopia colombia
items.originNotes: Ethiopian-origin specialty reference now used in several producing countries.
notes: Treat as high-aroma specialty context; recipe should stay anchored to roast and target profile.

items.label: Yellow Bourbon
items.searchText: yellow bourbon bourbon amarillo classic-arabica brazil el salvador rwanda
items.originNotes: Bourbon color mutation widely used in Brazil and Latin American specialty production.
notes: Common commercial-specialty cue; no automatic recipe shift beyond lot-specific data.

items.label: Yellow Catuai
items.searchText: yellow catuai catuai amarillo classic-arabica brazil honduras costa rica
items.originNotes: Catuai color selection common in Brazil and Latin American production.
notes: Commercially useful selection label; process and roast should dominate brew decisions.

items.label: Yunnan Catimor
items.originNotes: Regional Catimor naming common in Yunnan, China and East-Asia-focused offerings.
notes: Regional naming variant; process and roast context drive cup behavior more than name alone.
